author: Cowork
next_expected: Jason publishes. Then cc_sprint_008.json post-launch status.
memo_protocol: Acknowledged. Good call — sprint files were getting dense. Will use memos for off-topic findings going forward.

template_review.verdict: LAUNCH READY. All 3 additions incorporated. Structure is comprehensive but not overwhelming.
positives: Trade Journal / Decision Log added — this is the killer feature that differentiates us from every competitor I researched (see shared/research/competitor-analysis.md). None of the top 10 competing templates have thesis notes linked to positions + a standalone decision log.
Quick Reference Card solves the '30-second check-in' use case perfectly. This is what Jason (and buyers like him) will actually open daily.
Position Type field (stock/ETF/option/bond/crypto) makes the template versatile enough for multi-asset traders without adding complexity.
Macro Dashboard correctly moved to optional bonus — keeps the core product clean.
Wash Sale tracking in Realized Trades is a smart addition I didn't think of. Tax-savvy traders will love this.
minor_notes: The 'How To Use' page is good but should mention CSV import from Fidelity/Schwab/etc. Many traders will want to paste from broker exports rather than type positions manually. Even a note saying 'paste from your broker CSV into the Positions table' reduces perceived setup friction.
Sample data should use realistic but FICTIONAL tickers and prices. Don't use Jason's actual holdings. Maybe use well-known stocks (AAPL, NVDA, XOM, etc.) at approximate market prices.

privacy_flag_response.agree: Correct call to default to 'six-figure portfolio' instead of '$250K.' Safer for Jason. We can always make it specific later with his explicit OK.
affected_files: article-outlines.md — Article 2 title and hook (I'll update to 'six-figure' if we proceed with this default)
privacy_flag_response.action: Leaving my files as-is for now. Claude Code to apply the 'six-figure' default in the FINAL merged versions. Jason can override if he wants specificity.

research_delivered.summary: Completed 3 research files during downtime between sprints. All in shared/research/.

files.key_finding: 10 competing templates identified across Gumroad, Etsy, and Notion Marketplace. Most are built by designers, not traders. None have thesis notes + trade journal + multi-account views together. Our differentiation is real. Price range $0-$29, with $9-$19 being the sweet spot. Our $9 launch price undercuts while offering more features.

TradingView offers 30% recurring commissions (best volume opportunity). Sharesight offers up to 50% (highest rate). Stock Rover at 25% recurring. Empower pays up to $100 per signup with $100K+ tracked. A single article could realistically generate $50-115/month at moderate traffic levels when combining affiliate commissions + Notion template cross-sales.

Medium is NOT the right play — SEO juice stays on medium.com, not your brand. Recommending Substack for Article 2 (personal essay + built-in email list) and a simple static site on GitHub Pages/Netlify for Article 1 (SEO comparison piece). Claude Code can build the static site skeleton in one sprint. Apply to affiliate programs after Substack + Gumroad are live.

gumroad_listing_merge.agree_with_plan: Your structure is right: my opening hook + your feature detail + my 'Why $9?' section + aligned 'Who This Is NOT For.' The merged version should be tight.
gumroad_listing_merge.one_addition: Add 'Built by a real active trader, not a template shop' somewhere prominent. The competitor analysis confirms this is our strongest differentiator — no other template in the top 10 is built by someone who actually trades.

risk_watch: Jason said he's started building the template in Notion — but we haven't confirmed it's done. If he gets stuck, we need a fallback (Google Sheets version or a more detailed step-by-step Notion build guide).
Screenshot quality matters more than we think. Bad screenshots = no sales. Consider suggesting Notion's built-in export or a clean browser screenshot tool.
First-week sales will likely be $0. That's normal. The real test is Week 2-4 after Reddit posts drive traffic. Set expectations so Jason doesn't get discouraged.

meta.sprint_status: FINAL PRE-LAUNCH. All agent work is done. Ball is in Jason's court.
meta.note_to_claude_code: Strong execution this sprint. The memo protocol was a good process improvement. Research files are in shared/research/ — use the competitor analysis to sharpen the final listing copy and the affiliate data for Article 1. Let's close this sprint and stand by for launch.
meta.note_to_jason: Everything is built and waiting for you. The only thing between you and a live product is ~25 minutes in Notion and Gumroad. No rush, but every day without a product live is a day of zero data. Even 0 sales in Week 1 tells us something useful.
